perturbation.original_text: 1.13 "Conforming Product" means, with respect to the applicable Product, that, as of the date of delivery to Vericel or its designated Affiliate or contractor in accordance with Section 2.9(c) hereof, such Product (a) meets, and was Manufactured in accordance with, the applicable Specifications, Regulatory Standards (including cGMP where applicable) and the requirements set forth in the Quality Agreement, (b) is free from defects in materials and workmanship, (c) is not adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of the FD&C Act (or similar requirements of the countries for which the Product will be distributed), and (d) is not an article which may not, under the provisions of the FD&C Act, be introduced into interstate commerce.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.13 "Conforming Product" means, with respect to the applicable Product, that, as of the date of delivery to Vericel or its designated Affiliate or contractor in accordance with Section 2.9(c) hereof, such Product (a) substantially meets, and was Manufactured in general accordance with, the applicable Specifications, Regulatory Standards (including cGMP where applicable) and the requirements set forth in the Quality Agreement, (b) may have minor defects in materials and workmanship, (c) may be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of the FD&C Act (or similar requirements of the countries for which the Product will be distributed), and (d) is an article which may not, under the provisions of the FD&C Act, be introduced into interstate commerce.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Conforming Product' requires strict adherence to specifications and absence of defects/adulteration. The modified definition introduces ambiguity by stating 'substantially meets,' 'minor defects,' and 'may be adulterated,' thus creating a contradiction. This affects how product acceptance criteria are interpreted in later sections like 2.12 (Non-Conforming Product).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.12 Non-Conforming Product. (a) Rejection Notice. Unless otherwise mutually agreed by the Parties in writing, within [***] after receipt of a delivery of Product hereunder, Vericel shall give MediWound written notice of rejection ("Rejection Notice") (i) if the Product does not constitute Conforming Product ("Non-Conforming Product") or (ii) of any shortage in quantity of such delivery of Product. Any such Rejection Notice provided with respect to any quantity of Product shall be deemed to apply to the full Batch of such Product unless otherwise specified by Vericel. Vericel shall be deemed to have accepted such shipment of Product as Conforming Product and any shortage in quantity if it does not provide Rejection Notice within [***] after receipt of delivery describing the reasons for such rejections in reasonable detail, provided, however, that such [***] period shall not apply to any Latent Defects, in which case Vericel shall notify MediWound of any such failure as soon as reasonably possible, but in any event within [***] after the Latent Defect is confirmed by Vericel and prior to expiration of the shelf-life for such Product. (b) Disputes. In the event that MediWound disagrees with Vericel's claim that Product fails to constitute Conforming Product, then the Parties shall promptly attempt to resolve such dispute. If the Parties cannot resolve such dispute, a sample of such Product shall be submitted by MediWound and Vericel to a mutually agreeable qualified Third Party laboratory for testing against the applicable Specifications, Regulatory Standards and other standards and controls in the Quality Agreement and the test results obtained by such laboratory shall be final and controlling (absent manifest error). Test results must be furnished to both Parties within [***] of concluding such testing. The fees and expenses of such laboratory testing and any obsolescence due to short dating shall be borne entirely by the Party whose original Product analysis was in error. (c) Remedy. On receipt of Vericel's Rejection Notice pursuant to Section 2.12(a), subject to Section 2.12(b), MediWound shall, [***] (except if such Non-Conforming Product is due to MediWound's gross negligence or willful misconduct): (i) deliver the appropriate shortage quantities of Conforming Product as promptly as possible, at no additional cost or expense (including, without limitation, freight costs) to Vericel; (ii) replace the Non-Conforming Product with Conforming Product as promptly as possible, at no additional cost or expense (including, without limitation, freight costs) to Vericel; or (iii) promptly grant Vericel a credit in an amount equal to the amount paid or payable by Vericel with respect to reasonable out of pocket expenses directly associated with the Non-Conforming Product to the extent applicable (e.g. shipment costs, destruction fees, and restocking fees) and any such shortage or Non-Conforming Product, including, without limitation, but solely in the case of Non-Conforming Product, expenses associated with destruction or return at MediWound's instruction. This subsection (iii) shall additionally apply in the event Vericel elects as its option the foregoing (i) or (ii), as applicable, and such delivery or replace of Product thereunder is not practicable within a reasonable period of time (as reasonably determined by MediWound).
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 1.13 introduces ambiguity and directly contradicts the requirements for a 'Conforming Product' as referenced in Section 2.12. The original definition required strict adherence to specifications and absence of defects or adulteration. The changed definition allows for 'minor defects' and the product 'may be adulterated.' This directly clashes with Section 2.12, which allows for rejection of 'Non-Conforming Product,' defined as product that 'does not constitute Conforming Product.' The change introduces a level of subjectivity that makes it difficult to determine whether a product is, in fact, 'Conforming Product' as envisioned in Section 2.12, creating a strong in-text contradiction because the criteria for rejection in Section 2.12 is now unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 2.9 Delivery. (a) Shipping. MediWound shall only ship Products that have been Manufactured and released in accordance with the Specifications. Unless agreed in advance by Vericel and MediWound in writing, MediWound shall not ship (or permit such Third Party packager to ship) any Products prior to approval and release by MediWound in accordance with the Quality Agreement and Regulatory Standards.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.9 Delivery. (a) Shipping. MediWound shall endeavor to ship Products that have been Manufactured and released in general accordance with the Specifications. Unless agreed in advance by Vericel and MediWound in writing, MediWound may ship (or permit such Third Party packager to ship) any Products prior to approval and release by MediWound in accordance with the Quality Agreement and Regulatory Standards, if there is a reasonable justification for such shipping.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, MediWound was strictly held to shipping products manufactured and released in accordance with specifications. Now, the inserted perturbations changes this to 'endeavor to ship' and 'general accordance', effectively weakening the obligation. Additionally, allowing shipment prior to official approval undermines quality control processes defined earlier. These changes will create in-text contradictions on how the product will be considered for when is conforming as per 1.13
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.13 "Conforming Product" means, with respect to the applicable Product, that, as of the date of delivery to Vericel or its designated Affiliate or contractor in accordance with Section 2.9(c) hereof, such Product (a) substantially meets, and was Manufactured in general accordance with, the applicable Specifications, Regulatory Standards (including cGMP where applicable) and the requirements set forth in the Quality Agreement, (b) may have minor defects in materials and workmanship, (c) may be adulterated or misbranded within the meaning of the FD&C Act (or similar requirements of the countries for which the Product will be distributed), and (d) is an article which may not, under the provisions of the FD&C Act, be introduced into interstate commerce.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation regarding the quality and conformity of the products. The original text in 2.9(a) mandates that MediWound ships products manufactured and released strictly in accordance with the Specifications. The changed text weakens this obligation by stating that MediWound shall 'endeavor to ship' products manufactured and released in 'general accordance' with the specifications and allows shipment prior to approval, which contradicts the definition of 'Conforming Product' in section 1.13, which requires that the product 'substantially meets' and 'was Manufactured in general accordance with' the applicable specifications as of the date of delivery. The changes in 2.9(a) create an ambiguity on whether a product shipped before approval is considered a 'Conforming Product' under 1.13, thus creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.3 Product Warranty. MediWound represents and warrants to Vericel that, at the time of delivery of the given Product to the Delivery Site pursuant to Section 2.9(c), such Product so delivered pursuant to this Agreement will constitute Conforming Product and, except with respect to Section 2.13, will have a shelf life equal to or exceeding the Minimum Shelf Life (the "Product Warranty").
perturbation.changed_text: 6.3 Product Warranty. MediWound represents and warrants to Vericel that, at the time of delivery of the given Product to the Delivery Site pursuant to Section 2.9(c), such Product so delivered pursuant to this Agreement will generally constitute Conforming Product and, except with respect to Section 2.13, will have a shelf life approximating the Minimum Shelf Life (the "Product Warranty").
perturbation.explanation: The original Product Warranty guarantees that Products delivered will be 'Conforming Product' (strictly adhering to specifications) and meet a 'Minimum Shelf Life'. The modified version changes this to 'generally constitute Conforming Product' and 'approximating the Minimum Shelf Life,' thereby weakening these guarantees and creating ambiguity regarding acceptable deviations. Now in 2.12 Rejection Notice for Non-Conforming Product, it will be hard to decide what is actually a non-conforming product
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.12 Non-Conforming Product. (a) Rejection Notice. Unless otherwise mutually agreed by the Parties in writing, within [***] after receipt of a delivery of Product hereunder, Vericel shall give MediWound written notice of rejection ("Rejection Notice") (i) if the Product does not constitute Conforming Product ("Non-Conforming Product") or (ii) of any shortage in quantity of such delivery of Product. Any such Rejection Notice provided with respect to any quantity of Product shall be deemed to apply to the full Batch of such Product unless otherwise specified by Vericel. Vericel shall be deemed to have accepted such shipment of Product as Conforming Product and any shortage in quantity if it does not provide Rejection Notice within [***] after receipt of delivery describing the reasons for such rejections in reasonable detail, provided, however, that such [***] period shall not apply to any Latent Defects, in which case Vericel shall notify MediWound of any such failure as soon as reasonably possible, but in any event within [***] after the Latent Defect is confirmed by Vericel and prior to expiration of the shelf-life for such Product. If the Parties cannot resolve such dispute, a sample of such Product shall be submitted by MediWound and Vericel to a mutually agreeable qualified Third Party laboratory for testing against the applicable Specifications, Regulatory Standards and other standards and controls in the Quality Agreement and the test results obtained by such laboratory shall be final and controlling (absent manifest error). On receipt of Vericel's Rejection Notice pursuant to Section 2.12(a), subject to Section 2.12(b), MediWound shall, [***] (except if such Non-Conforming Product is due to MediWound's gross negligence or willful misconduct): (i) deliver the appropriate shortage quantities of Conforming Product as promptly as possible, at no additional cost or expense (including, without limitation, freight costs) to Vericel; (ii) replace the Non-Conforming Product with Conforming Product as promptly as possible, at no additional cost or expense (including, without limitation, freight costs) to Vericel; or (iii) promptly grant Vericel a credit in an amount equal to the amount paid or payable by Vericel with respect to reasonable out of pocket expenses directly associated with the Non-Conforming Product to the extent applicable (e.g. shipment costs, destruction fees, and restocking fees) and any such shortage or Non-Conforming Product, including, without limitation, but solely in the case of Non-Conforming Product, expenses associated with destruction or return at MediWound's instruction. perturbation.justification: The original text guarantees 'Conforming Product' and adherence to 'Minimum Shelf Life'. The changed text weakens these guarantees to 'generally constitute Conforming Product' and 'approximating the Minimum Shelf Life.' This directly contradicts Section 2.12, which requires Vericel to provide a 'Rejection Notice' if the Product does not constitute 'Conforming Product'. The ambiguity introduced by the changed text makes it difficult to determine what constitutes a 'Non-Conforming Product' under Section 2.12, leading to a contradiction.
